name: a retrospectively-phrased preference still supersedes: preferences key on reported_at, not validity
description: Preferences are valid FROM when reported, and newest-report wins (docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md fact kinds: preference = 'from reported_at', newest-wins low-urgency). Note A records Ingrid prefers window seats (valid_from = its capture). Note B is captured later but phrased retrospectively ('Ingrid has ALWAYS preferred aisle seats') with an OLD valid_from (2015). For a state this old validity would land as history, but decide()'s key() special-cases kind=='preference' to use (reported_at, reported_at), ignoring valid_from. So the later-reported aisle preference wins the >= test and SUPERSEDES the window preference with a low-urgency fact_conflict, despite its backdated valid_from. Pins that the retrospective branch does NOT apply to preferences — a preference's backdated validity must not bury it as history.

steps.body: Actually Ingrid has always preferred aisle seats, ever since 2015.

facts.statement: Ingrid has always preferred aisle seats.
